domain:
In an academic institution, students enroll in various courses offered by the university. Each student is uniquely identified by a student code. An enrollment in the course requires the specific date on which it occurred for administrative purposes.

Courses are supported by Teaching Assistants (TAs), who are identified by their first and last names. A TA may assist in multiple different courses, and the specific role of a TA within a course is defined by an assignment, which describes the details and responsibilities assigned to them. Regarding courses logistics, they consist of one or more lab sections. Each section occurs at a specific location and time slot with start and end times.

questions.question:
Should details be modeled as a separate class associated with Assignment, or remain as a simple representation?

For example:
1. Details is a separate class with its own attributes. This allows users to query and filter by individual details properties.
2. Details is modeled as a simple representation in Assignment. This is suitable when details details are not needed.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The Details is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The details is a simple representation in Assignment.
questions.selected: Option 2: The details is a simple representation in Assignment.

questions.question:
Should the details in assignment be modeled as a an attribute which allows free text input, or as an enumeration with predefined literals?

For example:
1. Use details as string attribute in assignment, which allows any text value.
2. Use details as an enumeration which uses predefined values in literals: grader, lab instructor, tutor, recitation leader, office hours, administrative or proctor.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for details.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Enumeration details with predefined values: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for details.

questions.question:
Should responsibilities be modeled as a separate class associated with Assignment, or remain as a simple representation?

For example:
1. Responsibilities is a separate class with its own attributes. This allows users to query and filter by individual responsibilities properties.
2. Responsibilities is modeled as a simple representation in Assignment. This is suitable when responsibilities details are not needed.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The Responsibilities is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The responsibilities is a simple representation in Assignment.
questions.selected: Option 2: The responsibilities is a simple representation in Assignment.

questions.question:
Should the responsibilities in assignment be modeled as a an attribute which allows free text input, or as an enumeration with predefined literals?

For example:
1. Use responsibilities as string attribute in assignment, which allows any text value.
2. Use responsibilities as an enumeration which uses predefined values in literals: grading, lab supervision, recitation leading, office hours, exam proctoring, material preparation or mentoring.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for responsibilities.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Enumeration responsibilities with predefined values: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for responsibilities.

questions.question:
Should the relationship between Course and Assignment be modeled as a composition or as an association?

For example:
1. Use composition, Assignment would depend on Course for its existence.
2. Use association, Assignment could exist independently of Course, and can also be linked to multiple courses.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Assignment depends on Course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Assignment exists independently of Course.
questions.selected: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Assignment depends on Course.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 1: Composition is needed because class Assignment depends on Course.

The domain states an assignment "defines the specific role of a TA within a course," so an Assignment is tied to and dependent on a particular Course for its existence rather than being an independent entity.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Course in the relationship with Assignment?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an assignment can exist without any associated course.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an assignment must have at least one associated course.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Assignment can exist without Course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Assignment must have at least one Course.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Assignment must have at least one Course.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of TeachingAssistant in the relationship with Assignment?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an assignment can exist without any associated teaching assistant.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an assignment must have at least one associated teaching assistant.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Assignment can exist without TeachingAssistant.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Assignment must have at least one TeachingAssistant.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Assignment can exist without TeachingAssistant.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Assignment in the relationship with TeachingAssistant?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a teaching assistant can exist without any associated assignment.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a teaching assistant must have at least one associated assignment.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, TeachingAssistant can exist without Assignment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, TeachingAssistant must have at least one Assignment.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, TeachingAssistant must have at least one Assignment.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Course in the relationship with Enrollment?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an enrollment can exist without any associated course.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an enrollment must have at least one associated course.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Enrollment can exist without Course.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Enrollment must have at least one Course.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Enrollment can exist without Course.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Enrollment in the relationship with Course?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a course can exist without any associated enrollment.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a course must have at least one associated enrollment.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Course can exist without Enrollment.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Course must have at least one Enrollment.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Course must have at least one Enrollment.

questions.question:
Should the relationship between Student and Enrollment be modeled as a composition or as an association?

For example:
1. Use composition, Enrollment would depend on Student for its existence.
2. Use association, Enrollment could exist independently of Student, and can also be linked to multiple students.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Enrollment depends on Student.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Association is sufficient because class Enrollment exists independently of Student.
questions.selected: Option 1: Composition is needed because class Enrollment depends on Student.
questions.llm_raw_response:
Option 1: Composition is needed because class Enrollment depends on Student.

Enrollment represents a specific student’s act of enrolling in a course (including the enrollment date), so an Enrollment cannot meaningfully exist without the associated Student.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Student in the relationship with Enrollment?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, an enrollment can exist without any associated student.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, an enrollment must have at least one associated student.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Enrollment can exist without Student.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality, Enrollment must have at least one Student.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Enrollment can exist without Student.

questions.question:
Should time slot be modeled as a separate class associated with LabSection, or as a simple representation within LabSection?

For example:
1. Time slot is a separate class with attributes: start time and end time. This allows users to query and filter by individual time slot properties.
2. Time slot is modeled as a simple represntation in LabSection. This is suitable when time slot details are not needed.
Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: The TimeSlot is a separate class with its own attributes.
questions.option_2: Option 2: The time slot is a simple representation in LabSection.
questions.selected: Option 2: The time slot is a simple representation in LabSection.

questions.question:
Should the time slot in lab section be modeled as a an attribute which allows free text input, or as an enumeration with predefined literals?

For example:
1. Use time slot as string attribute in lab section, which allows any text value.
2. Use time slot as an enumeration which uses predefined values in literals: morning, midday, afternoon, evening, night or custom (explicit start/end).

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for TimeSlot.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Enumeration TimeSlot with predefined values: literal 1, literal 2 and literal 3.
questions.selected: Option 1: String attribute that allows any text value for TimeSlot.

questions.question:
Should Enrollment be modeled as an association class linking Course and Student, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: Enrollment exists only as part of the relationship between Course and Student. A enrollment cannot exist without the link between Course and Student.
2. Regular class: Enrollment is a class that has links to Course and Student. In this case multiple enrollments can exist for every Course and Student.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, Enrollment as association class linking Course and Student.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, Enrollment as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, Enrollment as association class linking Course and Student.

questions.question:
Should Assignment be modeled as an association class linking Course and TeachingAssistant, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: Assignment exists only as part of the relationship between Course and TeachingAssistant. A assignment cannot exist without the link between Course and TeachingAssistant.
2. Regular class: Assignment is a class that has links to Course and TeachingAssistant. In this case multiple assignments can exist for every Course and TeachingAssistant.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, Assignment as association class linking Course and TeachingAssistant.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, Assignment as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 1: Association class, Assignment as association class linking Course and TeachingAssistant.
